title: 2010 Trek Gary Fisher
description:
Post for seat pole damaged. 
A man my age was yelling at it with the seat off, and I asled him what was wrong. He said the seat wouldn't tighten. I observed the frame damage and figured I could make it at least RIDEABLE for him. We walk it back to my apartment, and he starts getting all weirded out, as I'm working on it. He says he better leave, and I say that it's literally rideable until he can find a repair shop. He says "It's your bike now." 
Which leads me to believe it's stolen. I called Eugene, OR PD, and the serial numbers I gave them weren't showing up anywhere.

title: 2021 Carbon Master-handbike Handbike-recumbent
description: This is actually a customized handbike for a full time manual wheelchair athlete. The full carbon frame has decals that read “Carbon Master” in green and white. The bike has one training (clincher) wheel in the front, size 26’ (may still have a label on the wheel that says “Kara”). It has two small 20’ rear wheels. The bike has an SRM power adapter in the cranks and SRAM electronic shifting. Braking is v-brake style, one that is attached to the hand cranks and an emergency break underneath the fork. The handbike also has a thin black pad in the bucket (the part you lay on). The back of the bucket where your head would rest has a large hole in the frame to carry water etc.
